labeling_rule: Facts are chunk-scoped and exhaustive. A predicted claim matches a gold fact when it is grounded in the same evidence_span / numeric_facts. Multiple predicted claims may map to one fact (many-to-one). subject is correct if it is in allowed_subjects. direction is derived from current vs prior values. magnitude is derived from magnitude_bands for numeric facts. Numeric exactness, direction, magnitude, and subject are scored as separate axes, not as one exact-match tuple.

chunk-01-revenue-overview.canonical_statement: Compute & Networking revenue increased 88% year over year to $74.550 billion in Q1 FY2027.
chunk-01-revenue-overview.evidence_span: Compute & Networking revenue of $74.550 billion compared with $39.589 billion, a year-over-year increase of $34.961 billion, or 88%

chunk-01-revenue-overview.canonical_statement: Graphics revenue increased 58% year over year to $7.065 billion in Q1 FY2027.
chunk-01-revenue-overview.evidence_span: Graphics revenue was $7.065 billion compared with $4.473 billion, a year-over-year increase of $2.592 billion, or 58%

chunk-01-revenue-overview.canonical_statement: Total revenue increased 85% year over year to $81.615 billion in Q1 FY2027.
chunk-01-revenue-overview.evidence_span: Total revenue was $81.615 billion compared with $44.062 billion, an increase of $37.553 billion, or 85%

chunk-01-revenue-overview.canonical_statement: Compute & Networking operating income increased 142% year over year to $53.335 billion in Q1 FY2027.
chunk-01-revenue-overview.evidence_span: Compute & Networking operating income was $53.335 billion compared with $22.054 billion, up 142%

chunk-01-revenue-overview.canonical_statement: Graphics operating income increased 79% year over year to $2.941 billion in Q1 FY2027.
chunk-01-revenue-overview.evidence_span: Graphics operating income was $2.941 billion compared with $1.640 billion, up 79%

chunk-01-revenue-overview.canonical_statement: Total reportable segment operating income increased 138% year over year to $56.276 billion in Q1 FY2027.
chunk-01-revenue-overview.evidence_span: Total reportable segment operating income was $56.276 billion compared with $23.694 billion, up 138%

chunk-02-data-center-demand.canonical_statement: Compute & Networking revenue growth was driven by Data Center products, Blackwell systems, and networking demand.
chunk-02-data-center-demand.evidence_span: growth in Data Center products, driven by the ramp of our Blackwell systems and demand for our InfiniBand, Spectrum-X Ethernet, and NVLink solutions

chunk-02-data-center-demand.canonical_statement: Graphics revenue growth was driven by sales of Blackwell architecture.
chunk-02-data-center-demand.evidence_span: Graphics revenue. The year-over-year increase in the first quarter of fiscal year 2027 was driven by sales of our Blackwell architecture

chunk-02-data-center-demand.canonical_statement: Compute & Networking operating income growth was helped by revenue growth and non-recurrence of a prior $4.5 billion H20 charge.
chunk-02-data-center-demand.evidence_span: Compute & Networking segment operating income in the first quarter of fiscal year 2027 was driven by the growth in revenue and the non-recurrence of a $4.5 billion charge associated with H20 excess inventory and purchase obligations

numeric_facts.metric: H20 excess inventory and purchase obligations charge

chunk-02-data-center-demand.canonical_statement: Graphics segment operating income growth was driven by revenue growth.
chunk-02-data-center-demand.evidence_span: Graphics segment operating income in the first quarter of fiscal year 2027 was driven by the growth in revenue

chunk-03-customer-concentration.canonical_statement: NVIDIA revenue is concentrated among a limited number of direct and indirect customers and the trend may continue.
chunk-03-customer-concentration.evidence_span: Our revenue is concentrated among a limited number of direct and indirect customers and this trend may continue

chunk-03-customer-concentration.canonical_statement: Three direct customers represented 21%, 17%, and 16% of Q1 FY2027 total revenue, primarily from Compute & Networking.
chunk-03-customer-concentration.evidence_span: three direct customers represented 21%, 17%, and 16% of total revenue, all primarily attributable to the Compute & Networking segment

chunk-04-geographic-mix.canonical_statement: NVIDIA generates significant revenue from a limited number of indirect customers, some individually 10% or more of revenue.
chunk-04-geographic-mix.evidence_span: We generate a significant amount of our revenue from a limited number of indirect customers, some individually representing 10% or more of our revenue

chunk-04-geographic-mix.canonical_statement: One AI research and deployment company contributed a meaningful amount of revenue through cloud services purchases.
chunk-04-geographic-mix.evidence_span: one AI research and deployment company contributed to a meaningful amount of our revenue by purchasing cloud services from our customers

chunk-04-geographic-mix.canonical_statement: Revenue from customers headquartered outside the United States decreased from 42% to 22% of total revenue.
chunk-04-geographic-mix.evidence_span: Revenue from sales to customers headquartered outside the United States accounted for 22% of total revenue for the first quarter of fiscal year 2027 and 42% for the first quarter of fiscal year 2026

chunk-05-gross-margin.canonical_statement: Gross margin increased to 74.9% from 60.5% because the prior year included an H20 inventory charge.
chunk-05-gross-margin.evidence_span: Gross margin increased to 74.9% for the first quarter of fiscal year 2027 compared to 60.5% for the first quarter of fiscal year 2026

chunk-05-gross-margin.canonical_statement: The prior year's $4.5 billion H20 excess inventory and purchase obligations charge depressed gross margin.
chunk-05-gross-margin.evidence_span: prior year's $4.5 billion charge associated with H20 excess inventory and purchase obligations

numeric_facts.metric: H20 excess inventory and purchase obligations charge

chunk-05-gross-margin.canonical_statement: Inventory and excess purchase obligation provisions fell to $1.1 billion from $5.3 billion.
chunk-05-gross-margin.evidence_span: Provisions for inventory and excess inventory purchase obligations totaled $1.1 billion and $5.3 billion for the first quarter of fiscal years 2027 and 2026, respectively

numeric_facts.metric: inventory and excess purchase obligation provisions
inventory and excess purchase obligation provisions

chunk-05-gross-margin.canonical_statement: Previously reserved inventory sales and excess purchase obligation settlements released $103 million and $436 million of provisions.
chunk-05-gross-margin.evidence_span: Sales of previously reserved inventory and settlements of excess inventory purchase obligations resulted in a provision release of $103 million and $436 million, respectively

numeric_facts.metric: excess inventory purchase obligations provision release

chunk-06-operating-expenses.canonical_statement: Research and development expense increased 58% year over year to $6.321 billion in Q1 FY2027.
chunk-06-operating-expenses.evidence_span: Research and development expense was $6.321 billion for the three months ended April 26, 2026, compared with $3.989 billion for the three months ended April 27, 2025, an increase of $2.332 billion, or 58%

chunk-06-operating-expenses.canonical_statement: Sales, general and administrative expense increased 25% year over year to $1.300 billion in Q1 FY2027.
chunk-06-operating-expenses.evidence_span: Sales, general and administrative expense was $1.300 billion compared with $1.041 billion, an increase of $259 million, or 25%

chunk-06-operating-expenses.canonical_statement: Total operating expenses increased 52% year over year to $7.621 billion in Q1 FY2027.
chunk-06-operating-expenses.evidence_span: Total operating expenses were $7.621 billion compared with $5.030 billion, an increase of $2.591 billion, or 52%

chunk-06-operating-expenses.canonical_statement: Compute and infrastructure costs rose 112% year over year, a primary driver of R&D growth.
chunk-06-operating-expenses.evidence_span: a 112% increase in compute and infrastructure

chunk-06-operating-expenses.canonical_statement: Compensation and benefits rose 31% year over year, reflecting employee growth.
chunk-06-operating-expenses.evidence_span: a 31% increase in compensation and benefits

chunk-06-operating-expenses.canonical_statement: Engineering development materials costs rose 204% year over year for new product introductions.
chunk-06-operating-expenses.evidence_span: a 204% increase in engineering development materials for new product introductions

chunk-07-investment-gains-tax.canonical_statement: Interest income increased to $540 million from $515 million.
chunk-07-investment-gains-tax.evidence_span: Interest income was $540 million for the first quarter of fiscal year 2027 compared with $515 million for the first quarter of fiscal year 2026

chunk-07-investment-gains-tax.canonical_statement: Interest expense increased to $102 million from $63 million.
chunk-07-investment-gains-tax.evidence_span: Interest expense was $102 million compared with $63 million

chunk-07-investment-gains-tax.canonical_statement: Other income swung to $15.929 billion from a prior other expense of $180 million.
chunk-07-investment-gains-tax.evidence_span: Other income was $15.929 billion compared with other expense of $180 million

chunk-07-investment-gains-tax.canonical_statement: Total other income, net, increased to $16.367 billion from $272 million.
chunk-07-investment-gains-tax.evidence_span: Total other income, net, was $16.367 billion compared with $272 million

chunk-07-investment-gains-tax.canonical_statement: Other income change was primarily driven by $13.4 billion of publicly-held equity gains and $2.6 billion of non-marketable equity gains.
chunk-07-investment-gains-tax.evidence_span: primarily driven by unrealized gains on investments in publicly-held equity securities of $13.4 billion and non-marketable equity securities of $2.6 billion

chunk-07-investment-gains-tax.canonical_statement: Income tax expense increased to $11.6 billion from $3.1 billion.
chunk-07-investment-gains-tax.evidence_span: Income tax expense was $11.6 billion and $3.1 billion for the first quarter of fiscal years 2027 and 2026, respectively

chunk-08-capital-return.canonical_statement: NVIDIA repurchased 108 million shares for $20.2 billion in Q1 FY2027.
chunk-08-capital-return.evidence_span: we repurchased 108 million shares of our common stock for $20.2 billion

chunk-08-capital-return.canonical_statement: NVIDIA had $38.5 billion of remaining repurchase authorization as of April 26, 2026.
chunk-08-capital-return.evidence_span: we were authorized, subject to certain specifications, to repurchase up to $38.5 billion of our common stock

chunk-08-capital-return.canonical_statement: The board approved an additional $80.0 billion share repurchase authorization.
chunk-08-capital-return.evidence_span: our Board of Directors approved an additional $80.0 billion in share repurchase authorization

chunk-08-capital-return.canonical_statement: NVIDIA paid $243 million of cash dividends in Q1 FY2027.
chunk-08-capital-return.evidence_span: We paid cash dividends to our shareholders of $243 million during the first quarter of fiscal year 2027

chunk-08-capital-return.canonical_statement: NVIDIA increased its quarterly cash dividend from $0.01 per share to $0.25 per share.
chunk-08-capital-return.evidence_span: we increased our quarterly cash dividend from $0.01 per share to $0.25 per share

chunk-09-h200-china-licenses.canonical_statement: The US government granted licenses allowing small H200 shipments to specific China-based customers.
chunk-09-h200-china-licenses.evidence_span: the USG granted licenses that would allow us to ship small amounts of H200 products to specific China-based customers

chunk-09-h200-china-licenses.canonical_statement: NVIDIA has not generated revenue under the H200 licensing program to date.
chunk-09-h200-china-licenses.evidence_span: To date, we have not generated any revenue under the H200 licensing program

chunk-09-h200-china-licenses.canonical_statement: NVIDIA does not know whether H200 imports will be allowed into China.
chunk-09-h200-china-licenses.evidence_span: do not yet know whether any imports will be allowed into China

chunk-09-h200-china-licenses.canonical_statement: Licensed H200 shipments must go through a US inspection process before shipment.
chunk-09-h200-china-licenses.evidence_span: The license requires that the H200s go through an inspection process in the United States prior to any shipment to the customer

chunk-09-h200-china-licenses.canonical_statement: Any H200 shipped under the licensing program will face a 25% tariff upon US importation.
chunk-09-h200-china-licenses.evidence_span: any H200 shipped under the new licensing program will be subject to a 25% tariff upon importation into the United States

chunk-09-h200-china-licenses.canonical_statement: NVIDIA may be unable to pass H200 tariffs to customers if it sells licensed products into China.
chunk-09-h200-china-licenses.evidence_span: we may not be able to pass along all or any of the tariff to our customers

chunk-09-h200-china-licenses.canonical_statement: Licensed China sales could expose NVIDIA to litigation, increased costs, and harmed competitive position.
chunk-09-h200-china-licenses.evidence_span: may be subject to litigation, increased costs, and a harmed competitive position

chunk-10-export-controls-risk.canonical_statement: China export controls are complex and cover processing performance, performance density, interconnect bandwidth, and memory bandwidth.
chunk-10-export-controls-risk.evidence_span: The export controls applicable to China are complex and address a variety of parameters, including total processing performance, performance density, interconnect bandwidth, and memory bandwidth

chunk-10-export-controls-risk.canonical_statement: NVIDIA cannot create and deliver a competitive China data center product approved by both US and Chinese governments.
chunk-10-export-controls-risk.evidence_span: we are unable to create and deliver a competitive product for China's data center market that receives approval from both the USG and the Chinese government

chunk-10-export-controls-risk.canonical_statement: NVIDIA was able to ship uncontrolled gaming and workstation GPUs to China.
chunk-10-export-controls-risk.evidence_span: we were able to ship uncontrolled products to China, such as gaming and workstation GPUs

chunk-10-export-controls-risk.canonical_statement: NVIDIA was effectively foreclosed from competing in China's data center computing market.
chunk-10-export-controls-risk.evidence_span: we were effectively foreclosed from competing in China's data center computing market

chunk-10-export-controls-risk.canonical_statement: NVIDIA's foreclosure helped competitors build larger developer and customer ecosystems worldwide.
chunk-10-export-controls-risk.evidence_span: our effective foreclosure helped competitors build larger developer and customer ecosystems to challenge us worldwide

chunk-10-export-controls-risk.canonical_statement: If NVIDIA cannot return with an approved China data center system, the lost opportunity and competitor benefit will materially harm business, operating results, and financial condition.
chunk-10-export-controls-risk.evidence_span: our lost opportunity and the benefit to competitors will have a material and adverse impact on our business, operating results, and financial condition
